summary.intent: Build a complete generic webhooks feature for Moltis that allows running AI agents triggered by GitHub, GitLab, Stripe, and other webhook providers.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented `moltis-webhooks` crate with 7 provider profiles, HTTP ingress endpoint, SQLite store, background worker with crash recovery, web UI settings page, documentation, and test tooling. PR #575 created and all review comments fixed.

repo: Settings sidebar nav icons are rendered via CSS `::before` pseudo-elements in `components.css` using `data-section` attribute selectors — NOT from an `icon` property in the JS sections array. This is the source of many 'black square' icon bugs.
The compiled `style.css` (Tailwind output) is gitignored; the `components.css` file in `assets/css/` is the one that must be modified for settings sidebar icons.
There are TWO style.css files: `src/assets/style.css` (Tailwind output, served) and `src/assets/css/style.css` (components, also served). The HTML loads the Tailwind one via versioned asset path.

Arc::get_mut() silently returns None if the Arc has already been cloned elsewhere, making it unreliable for post-construction field initialization. Use OnceLock for fields that must be set after Arc creation.
The i18n website build uses a string-replacement script in `website/scripts/build-i18n.mjs` that regenerates all language files from the English template.
Webhook endpoint URL must use server's public URL (ngrok/tailscale), not `window.location.origin`. The `preferredPublicBaseUrl` pattern exists in `onboarding-view.js` for this.
HTM (JSX-like) collapses whitespace between tags across newlines; use `${" "}` to force explicit spaces between inline elements and text.
The codebase has a pre-existing build error in `moltis-httpd` with vault/ssh_routes feature — unrelated to any changes.
CORS headers are needed on the webhook ingress endpoint for browser-based test tools (Hoppscotch web). The endpoint is at `/api/webhooks/ingest/` and needs `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`.

Dedup using body SHA-256 hash as fallback is too aggressive for generic webhooks — identical test payloads from curl always get deduplicated. Only dedup when sender provides explicit idempotency headers.
GatewayState uses `Arc<GatewayState>` pattern; fields cannot be mutated after Arc creation without OnceLock or passing through the constructor.

code.finding: Worker startup must drain unprocessed (queued/received) deliveries from the store to survive server restarts — crash recovery pattern.
TOCTOU gap when using two separate RwLocks for global and per-webhook rate limits. Fix: use single Mutex holding both sliding windows so check-and-record is atomic.
Byte-index string slicing like `&body_text[..500]` panics on multibyte UTF-8. Must use a safe truncation helper that walks back to char boundary.
Added `truncate_str(s, max_bytes)` helper that safely truncates UTF-8 strings to a byte limit without panicking on multibyte characters.
webhook_store, webhook_rate_limiter, webhook_worker_tx changed from Option<T> to OnceLock<T> to allow safe post-Arc initialization.
Auth secrets (auth_config, source_config) must be redacted via Webhook::redacted() before returning from list/get/create/update RPCs. Store keeps real values for ingress verification.
All settings sidebar nav icons defined here as `.settings-nav-item[data-section="NAME"]::before` with mask-image pointing to SVG files in icons/masks/.
Remote IP extraction for CIDR enforcement reads from X-Forwarded-For then X-Real-Ip headers. ConnectInfo extractor requires router-level setup, header approach works behind proxies.

workflow: Use Arc::get_mut() only immediately after Arc::new() before any clones; use OnceLock for fields that need late initialization on an already-shared Arc.
For browser-based webhook testing tools, CORS headers + OPTIONS preflight handler are required even for POST endpoints that otherwise don't need them.
When building background workers, always implement crash recovery (drain incomplete jobs from store on startup) — not just forward-looking dequeue.
Hoppscotch web cannot reach localhost due to CORS + self-signed cert + mixed content. The desktop app or curl are the correct tools for localhost webhook testing.
beads issues can be filed via `gh issue create` against the moltis repo for tracking.
GitHub PR review threads can be resolved via GraphQL mutation `resolveReviewThread` with the thread node ID.

friction: Arc::get_mut() silently failed (returned None), causing webhook_store to stay None and every ingress request to return 404. No warning was logged, so it was invisible until debugging.

Settings sidebar icon system is non-obvious — the `icon` property in JS sections array is unused; CSS components.css handles it via attribute selectors. This caused multiple 'black square' debugging iterations.
HTM whitespace collapsing caused 'withHoppscotch' to appear without a space — had to use explicit JSX whitespace hack.
OAuth token expired at session start, causing initial authentication error before the user could retry.
Hoppscotch web CORS error — the tool works on the web but can't reach localhost Moltis instances due to browser restrictions, making the initial recommendation misleading.
max-w-form class doesn't exist or isn't applied in the webhooks context — had to revert to full width after attempting to constrain the layout.
Tailwind-compiled style.css is gitignored so CSS class additions there don't persist — had to add icon to components.css instead.
Dedup was too aggressive using body SHA-256 hash fallback — repeated curl test commands all got deduplicated, making local testing frustrating.
open_items: Live-refresh chat UI when background workers append messages to already-open sessions (affects both webhooks and cron named sessions) — filed as beads issue moltis-2pu.
Website i18n translations for webhook feature text are placeholder English in all 9 language files — translations need to be added to build-i18n.mjs.
CIDR allowlist enforcement is implemented (IP extracted, field stored) but the actual CIDR matching/blocking logic in the ingress handler needs verification that it correctly rejects non-allowed IPs.
GitLab, Stripe, Linear, PagerDuty, Sentry profiles exist with auth and entity-key logic but lack integration tests equivalent to the GitHub profile tests.
Response action tools (post comments, merge PRs, issue refunds) are defined in the plan but not yet implemented as actual agent tools.
Named session per-entity locking (serialize events by entity key within a session) is described in plan but not implemented — concurrent events for same PR could interleave.
Source profile is described as read-only after creation in the plan but the edit UI doesn't enforce this restriction.
